sources_exist.note: Ten of twelve canonical occurrences were accessible, but the System2Thinking buy-multiple URL returned HTTP 406 and the Agent for the Future owner-age URL was inaccessible.

figures_match_sources.note: The accessible sources do not substantiate the 35% material-adoption estimate, 21%-to-56% e-closing analog, 40% title-agency owner age, count of three active consolidators, 4x/7x EV/EBITDA multiples, or 65%/35% revenue mix. The ALTA AI page also says 378 respondents, not the record's stated 631.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: Role fractions, pi_dist, and pi_moat are bounded and have concrete industry rationales, but t50_years=4 is derived from an unsupported 35% material-use conversion and an uncited 21%-to-56% analog. The LOW_CONFIDENCE flag is therefore unresolved.

rubric_consistent.note: All deterministic dataset inputs match exactly, and the ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL flag on B and S is a benign stored-precision issue. However, adoption timing and capture rely on evidence that measures any individual generative-AI use rather than material firm workflow adoption and on unsupported 65%/35% pricing weights, contrary to the rubric's required industry-specific evidence.

cross_checks_honest.note: The uplift, no-proxy, and PRESSURED terminal-value discussions are internally candid, but the pricing-deflation cross-check asserts that roughly 65% of revenue is protected premium split and 35% is market-priced without source support, making its claimed mapping to pi_dist/pi_moat unverified.

source_audits.note: The page is an undated historical table of title-insurance underwriter mergers. It does not identify the three named agency acquirers as active in the last 24 months, document Shaddock as PE-backed, or support count=3 for this specific industry.
The cited page returned HTTP 406 and was not accessible for verification; therefore neither its purported 3-6.5 profit-multiplier range nor the normalized 4.0x EV/EBITDA estimate is auditable.

ALTA reports that 90% of 378 surveyed professionals had adopted generative-AI solutions regardless of formal workflow integration. It does not report 631 respondents, 33% daily plus 30% weekly use, 17% formal-policy prevalence, or 35% of firms materially using AI in core title-search/curative/processing workflows.

The release confirms FNF's October 2024 acquisition of First Nationwide Title Agency's commercial operations but discloses no price, EBITDA, or transaction multiple. It does not support public 10-12x, historical 6-9x, or a 7.0x platform exit multiple.

ALTA supports 61% of surveyed title/settlement companies offering some digital or remote closing and a transaction mix rising from 7% in 2021 to 10% in 2022. It does not report title-agency e-closing adoption rising from 21% in 2020 to 56% in 2023, which is the central pace used for t50.

The cited page was inaccessible, so the claimed 40% of independent-insurance-agency principals over 60 could not be verified; even if verified, it is an acknowledged cross-industry proxy rather than title-agency owner evidence.

The FAQ supports that title premiums are regulated in most states and that attorneys may separately charge title-search or closing fees. It does not support order-flow captivity, a 65% premium/35% settlement revenue mix, a 70-80% agency premium retention, or pi_dist=0.35.

The article supports that title leaders are investing in AI, automation, and efficiency and describes pressure on agent-underwriter partnerships. It does not establish the record's upstream order-flow ownership, revenue-line weights, regulator clawback mechanism, or pi_dist=0.35.

The article confirms Fannie Mae stopped considering the title-waiver pilot in August 2023 while continuing limited acceptance of attorney opinion letters, supporting the stated bypass-channel context.
The article confirms Freddie Mac expanded AOL eligibility, including condos and restrictive-covenant properties, and describes AOLs as a limited alternative to lender title insurance.
Fannie Mae states that AOLs are permitted in limited circumstances and that it has purchased more than 10,000 AOL-backed loans since 2009, directly supporting that portion of the moat rationale.

The FAQ establishes a one-time title premium, regulated promulgated/filed premiums in most states, and possible separate title-search/closing fees. It does not establish that agents retain 70-80% of premium or that total agency revenue is 65% regulated premium split and 35% settlement/escrow fees.

reasons: Re-run inputs.current_adoption_pct and inputs.t50_years with a firm-level measure of material AI use in core workflows; https://www.alta.org/news-and-publications/news/20240716-90-of-Title-Professionals-Already-Using-AI-Qualia-Survey-Shows measures any professional generative-AI use among 378 respondents, not 35% material firm adoption, and does not contain the other quoted frequencies.

Re-run inputs.historical_analogs using a URL that actually reports the 21%-to-56% agency adoption series or derive timing only from figures in https://www.alta.org/blog/post/alta-survey-digital-closings-increase-but-barriers-slow-adoption, keeping company-offering and transaction-share metrics distinct.

Replace inputs.buy_mult and inputs.exit_mult with accessible transaction evidence normalized to EV/EBITDA; the buy URL is inaccessible, and https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/fidelity-national-financial-acquires-first-nationwide-title-agency-302265920.html discloses no consideration, EBITDA, or multiple.

Re-run inputs.active_consolidators with dated evidence for each PE-backed platform actively doing agency add-ons in the last 24 months; https://www.alta.org/business-operations/title-insurance-mergers is an undated underwriter-merger table and does not support count=3.

Replace inputs.owners_60plus_pct with an accessible title-agency owner source or a clearly supported proxy whose limitations and resulting score uncertainty are evidenced; the cited Agent for the Future page could not be accessed.

Source the 70-80% agent premium retention and 65% regulated-premium/35% settlement-fee revenue split used in inputs.pi_dist, inputs.pricing_structure, and cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation; the cited ALTA FAQ supports regulation and separate fees but not those percentages.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, S
